frameworks.name: latent linear dynamical systems
frameworks.one_line: extended to contain distinct latent variables per cell class

frameworks.name: cell-type dynamical systems (CTDS)
frameworks.one_line: latent dynamical system with cell-class-specific latents and constrained emissions

core_question: Can latent dynamical models capture the functional roles of distinct cell classes in neural population recordings?
key_assumptions: neural populations contain multiple cell classes with distinct functional roles
biologically inspired constraints can be imposed on dynamics and emissions
cell-class identity is partially known from electrophysiology
manipulations: cell-type-specific latent structure vs unstructured latents
in-silico optogenetic perturbations of E and I populations
key_findings: CTDS achieves higher accuracy than a standard LDS
choice can be decoded from both E and I latents in FOF and ADS
in-silico perturbations replicate experimentally observed behavioral effects
CTDS can infer cell-type identity for neurons with unknown class labels
interpretations: incorporating cell-class structure improves latent dynamics models and predicts cell-specific perturbation effects
distinct cell classes carry decodable choice information during decision-making
